epigraph.text: He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also, he has put eternity into man's heart.
epigraph.quote: The normal law of error stands out in the experience of mankind as one of the broadest generalizations of natural philosophy.

content.text:
Having introduced the normal distribution in Chapter 1, we now 
develop it more rigorously and explore its properties.

content.content:
This requires showing $\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2/2} \, dx = \sqrt{2\pi}$, 
a famous result proved by Gauss using polar coordinates.

content.title: Properties of $\Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$
content.content:
If $X \sim \Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$:

1. $\E[X] = \mu$

2. $\Var(X) = \sigma^2$

3. The distribution is symmetric about $\mu$

4. Mean = Median = Mode = $\mu$

5. Inflection points occur at $x = \mu \pm \sigma$
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Linear Transformation of Normal
content.content:
If $X \sim \Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$ and $Y = aX + b$, then:
\[
 Y \sim \Normal(a\mu + b, \, a^2\sigma^2)
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Sum of Normals
content.content:
If $X_1 \sim \Normal(\mu_1, \sigma_1^2)$ and $X_2 \sim \Normal(\mu_2, \sigma_2^2)$ 
are **independent**, then:
\[
 X_1 + X_2 \sim \Normal(\mu_1 + \mu_2, \, \sigma_1^2 + \sigma_2^2)
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
This is remarkable: the sum of independent normals is again normal. 
This "closure property" helps explain the normal distribution's 
importance.

content.title: Combining Measurements
content.problem:
Two components in a system have lengths 
$X_1 \sim \Normal(10, 0.5^2)$ cm and $X_2 \sim \Normal(15, 0.8^2)$ cm. 
The total length is $T = X_1 + X_2$. Find $P(T > 26)$.

content.title: Moment Generating Function
content.content:
The **moment generating function** 
(MGF) of $X$ is:
\[
 M_X(t) = \E[e^{tX}]
\]
when this expectation exists.
